classification.reasoning: The patch introduces a new feature for date formatting in JMTE templates by adding a new NamedDateRenderer class and integrating it into existing providers. It also modifies existing binding classes to use the new provider. Most changes are additions of new files and exact matches, but there is a fuzzy match in ServerBindings.java indicating some formatting or minor structural differences. The patch does not involve deep structural refactoring or redesign, but it is more than trivial string replacements, so it fits TYPE_II complexity. The confidence is high due to mostly exact matches and new files, with a small fuzzy match. The token budget is moderate due to multiple files and new class implementations.
